title: OATH — Shared Definitions
description: Reusable JSON Schema definitions shared across NYC Office of Administrative Trials & Hearings (OATH) object schemas. Reconciles fields common to the OATH Open Data datasets (data.cityofnewyork.us — Hearings Division Case Status jz4z-kudi, Trials Division Case Status y3hw-z6bm), nyc.gov/site/oath, and the ECB Ticket Finder / Respond-to-a-Summons portal (a820-ecbticketfinder.nyc.gov). Includes the NYC borough/geography spine, the set of agencies that issue ECB summonses, and the Respondent, Charge, and ViolationLocation building blocks of an ECB summons.

Coordinates.description: WGS84 point derived from a violation or respondent address geocode.

IssuingAgency.description: The city agency that issued the ECB summons ('Issuing Agency'). OATH adjudicates; it does not issue. Common issuers below; free text allowed for others.
examples: DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND MENTAL HYGIENE
DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AND WORKER PROTECTION

Address.description: A generic street address (used for both respondent and violation locations).

Respondent.description: The party charged on the summons ('Respondent Last Name', 'Respondent Address (...)'). Business name or individual. OATH publishes limited respondent fields to Open Data.

facilityNumber.description: 'Respondent Address or Facility Number (For FDNY and DOB Tickets)'.

ViolationLocation.description: Where the violation occurred ('Violation Location (...)'). Carries the tax-lot spine (Borough/Block/Lot) for building-issued summonses.

Charge.description: A single charge on an ECB summons. The Hearings Division dataset carries up to ten parallel charge columns ('Charge #N: Code', 'Code Section', 'Code Description', 'Infraction Amount'); this schema normalizes one charge.

HearingStatus.description: 'Hearing Status' — where the summons sits in the adjudication lifecycle.

ComplianceStatus.description: 'Compliance Status' — whether the respondent has cured/complied.
